content.text:
All tests pass. I now have a complete and verified picture of the change. Let me compile my findings.

---

## Code Review: live title refresh via SSE

**Reviewed (full branch diff vs `origin/main`, code only):**
- `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts` — fires `conversations:title-updated` inside the `if (updated)` branch
- `packages/conversations/src/types.ts` — `TitleUpdatedEvent` interface
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/title-events.ts` — per-user SSE handler
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/plugin.ts` — route registration + manifest `subscribes`
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/title-events.ts` — client SSE consumer
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/use-title-events.ts` — React hook
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/session-store.ts` — `applyTitle`
- `packages/channel-web/src/App.tsx` — mount point
- 5 test files

**Verification performed:** `tsc --build` on both packages (clean, exit 0); all 12 channel-web title-events tests pass and all 15 conversations set-title tests pass (under the correct package vitest config — jsdom). Cross-plugin import scan: clean in product code.

---

### No high-confidence issues found.

I went through each of the eight enumerated risks and could not produce a finding at confidence ≥ 80. The implementation is correct on every point. Brief confirmations below, with the two I scrutinized hardest called out.

**1. SSE frame injection — SAFE.** `server/title-events.ts:109-113` writes `data: ${JSON.stringify({conversationId, title})}\n\n`. `JSON.stringify` escapes `"` → `\"` and any real newline → the two-character sequence `\n`, so a model-generated title containing `\n`, `\n\n`, or `"` cannot emit a literal newline byte and cannot forge a `\n\n` frame boundary. The client parser (`lib/title-events.ts:83-91`) splits on real `\n`, skips non-`data: ` lines, and `JSON.parse`s the remainder inside a try/catch — a malformed slice is dropped, not executed. Round-trip is sound.

**2. Cross-user isolation — SAFE.** `userId` is server-derived via `auth:require-user` → `result.user.id` (`title-events.ts:44-57`), never request-supplied. The subscriber filters `payload.userId !== userId` (line 103) and additionally type-guards `conversationId`/`title`. Tested at `__tests__/server/title-events.test.ts:140-147`.

**3. Subscriber / timer leak — SAFE.** `cleanup()` (`title-events.ts:68-76`) is idempotent (`if (closed) return` then `closed = true`), clears the keepalive interval, and unsubscribes by the unique per-connection `subKey`. It is wired to `stream.onClose` (line 78), to `safeWrite`'s catch (line 85), and to the keepalive write-failure catch (line 127). `bus.unsubscribe` matches on the key and is a no-op if absent (verified in `core/src/hook-bus.ts:173-185`). All teardown paths covered; disconnect-cleanup is tested (`title-events.test.ts:149-156`).

**4. Client reconnect — SAFE, bounded.** `lib/title-events.ts:35-58`: a 401 makes `resp.ok` false → throws → caught → backoff before the next attempt, so a persistent 401 reconnects at most every `maxDelayMs` (30s), never a tight loop. `attempt` resets to 0 only after a confirmed open (line 48), so backoff grows correctly across consecutive failures. `stop()` sets `stopped = true` and calls `controller?.abort()`, which aborts the in-flight fetch; the `if (stopped) return` checks at lines 54 and inside the `while` guard end the loop. Reconnect-after-end and onOpen-reset are tested (`title-events.test.ts:39-60`).

**5. applyTitle correctness — CORRECT.** `session-store.ts:142-150`: returns early (stable `state.sessions` reference) on unknown id and on unchanged title; otherwise `slice()` + replace the single index with a new object via `set()`. No mutation, no off-by-one. Reference-stability on both no-op paths is explicitly tested (`session-store-apply-title.test.ts:22-34`).

**6. Fire placement — CORRECT.** `conversations/src/plugin.ts:626-630` fires strictly inside `if (updated)` and `return`s immediately, so it never reaches the ifNull no-op (`updated=false`) or the post-write not-found re-check below. All three cases are tested in `set-title.test.ts:498-581` (fires on real change; not on ifNull no-op; not on not-found).

**7. AX invariants — SATISFIED.** No product-code cross-plugin import: `server/title-events.ts:26-30` and `lib/title-events.ts:8-11` each declare their own local `TitleUpdatedEvent`/`TitleEventFrame` duck-type; the only `@ax/conversations` imports in channel-web are in `__tests__/` harness composition, consistent with existing tests. Payload is domain-level (`conversationId`/`userId`/`title`) with no storage/transport vocabulary (invariant #1). Not half-wired: the route auto-registers in `server/plugin.ts:147-162` and the consumer (`useTitleEvents` in `App.tsx:134`) ships in the same PR (invariant #3). Note: the manifest schema (`core/src/plugin.ts`) has only `registers`/`calls`/`subscribes` — there is no `emits`/`fires` field, and other fired events (`chat:stream-chunk`, `chat:turn-end`) are likewise undeclared, so not declaring `conversations:title-updated` is consistent with the established convention, not a gap.

**8. Test coverage — GOOD, with one minor gap noted below.** Server handler (401 / isolation / frame format / disconnect-cleanup), client consumer (frame parse / junk-skip / reconnect / onOpen), `applyTitle` (3 cases incl. reference stability), the hook (mount/route/unmount), and the producer event (3 cases) are all covered. The new route's registration is verified via the 401-not-404 probe (`__tests__/server/plugin.test.ts`).

---

### Minor (below report threshold, noted for completeness, not blocking)

- **Minor (confidence ~55): no explicit test for the newline/quote injection case.** Risk #1 is the headline security property of this change and the round-trip is correct, but no test feeds a title like `"\n\ndata: forged"` end-to-end (or even just asserts the server frame for such a title). Existing tests use clean titles (`'Hello'`, `'One'`). Suggested add: in `__tests__/server/title-events.test.ts`, fire a title containing `\n\n` and `"` and assert the captured write contains exactly one `\n\n` (the frame terminator) and the escaped sequence, not a second blank-line boundary. Cheap regression guard for the property the design leans on.

- **Nit (confidence ~40): keepalive write-failure path doesn't `stream.close()`.** `title-events.ts:122-129` — the keepalive catch calls `cleanup()` but, unlike `safeWrite` (lines 84-90), does not attempt `stream.close()`. Functionally fine (a write that just threw means the socket is already gone, and `cleanup` stops the timer + unsubscribes, so no leak), and it mirrors the same asymmetry already present in `sse.ts:291-298`, so it's consistent with the existing pattern. Not worth changing.

Net: this is a clean, well-tested, correctly-scoped change. I'd merge it; the only thing I'd genuinely suggest is the newline-injection test as a guard on the property the whole design depends on.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Review the whole feature branch for the "live title refresh via SSE" change in the ax-next monorepo.

**Working directory:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/feat+live-title-refresh-sse`
**Scope:** the full branch diff vs base. Run `git diff origin/main...HEAD` to see all changes. Ignore the two `docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-*.md` files (they're the design + plan, reviewed already) — focus on the CODE changes in `packages/conversations/` and `packages/channel-web/`.

**What the change does:** `conversations:set-title` now fires a new in-process bus event `conversations:title-updated {conversationId,userId,title}` only when a title actually changes. A new per-user SSE route `GET /api/chat/title-events` (channel-web) subscribes to it, filters by the authenticated userId, and streams `{conversationId,title}` frames. A long-lived client consumer (`title-events.ts` + `use-title-events.ts`, mounted in `AppContent`) updates the sidebar `session-store` row in place (`applyTitle`) and resyncs via `bumpVersion()`/list() on connect. The existing client poll is unchanged.

**Scrutinize these specific risks (report concrete findings with file:line):**
1. **SSE frame injection** — a `title` is model-generated (untrusted). It's `JSON.stringify`'d into `data: {...}\n\n`. Confirm a title containing `\n`, `\n\n`, or `"` cannot forge/escape an SSE frame boundary. Check both the server write (`server/title-events.ts`) and the client parser (`lib/title-events.ts`).
2. **Cross-user isolation** — can a connection ever receive another user's title? Check the subscriber's `payload.userId === userId` filter and where `userId` comes from (must be server-derived via `auth:require-user`, never request-supplied).
3. **Subscriber / timer leak** — per-connection `bus.subscribe` must be unsubscribed and the keepalive interval cleared on client disconnect (`onClose`) and on write failure. Verify `cleanup()` covers all paths and is idempotent.
4. **Client reconnect** — the consumer's backoff/reconnect loop: any tight-loop/storm risk if the server 401s or the stream ends immediately? Does `stop()` actually abort the in-flight fetch and end the loop? Is `attempt` reset correctly on successful open?
5. **applyTitle correctness** — does it keep the `sessions` array reference stable on no-op (unknown id / unchanged title) to avoid needless re-renders? Off-by-one / mutation bugs?
6. **Fire placement** — `conversations:title-updated` must fire ONLY inside the `if (updated)` branch (real change), never on the ifNull no-op or not-found path.
7. **AX invariants** — no cross-plugin imports (channel-web must NOT import from @ax/conversations; it duck-types the payload); payload is transport/storage-agnostic; the event isn't half-wired (consumer ships same PR).
8. **Test coverage** — are the behaviors above actually tested? Any critical gap (e.g., no test for the newline-injection case, or no cleanup-on-disconnect test)?

Report findings grouped by severity (Critical / Important / Minor / Nit). For each, give file:line and a concrete suggested fix. If something is correct, say so briefly — don't invent issues. Be rigorous but don't rubber-stamp.

content.text:
All tests pass. I now have a complete and verified picture of the change. Let me compile my findings.

---

## Code Review: live title refresh via SSE

**Reviewed (full branch diff vs `origin/main`, code only):**
- `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts` — fires `conversations:title-updated` inside the `if (updated)` branch
- `packages/conversations/src/types.ts` — `TitleUpdatedEvent` interface
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/title-events.ts` — per-user SSE handler
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/plugin.ts` — route registration + manifest `subscribes`
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/title-events.ts` — client SSE consumer
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/use-title-events.ts` — React hook
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/session-store.ts` — `applyTitle`
- `packages/channel-web/src/App.tsx` — mount point
- 5 test files

**Verification performed:** `tsc --build` on both packages (clean, exit 0); all 12 channel-web title-events tests pass and all 15 conversations set-title tests pass (under the correct package vitest config — jsdom). Cross-plugin import scan: clean in product code.

---

### No high-confidence issues found.

I went through each of the eight enumerated risks and could not produce a finding at confidence ≥ 80. The implementation is correct on every point. Brief confirmations below, with the two I scrutinized hardest called out.

**1. SSE frame injection — SAFE.** `server/title-events.ts:109-113` writes `data: ${JSON.stringify({conversationId, title})}\n\n`. `JSON.stringify` escapes `"` → `\"` and any real newline → the two-character sequence `\n`, so a model-generated title containing `\n`, `\n\n`, or `"` cannot emit a literal newline byte and cannot forge a `\n\n` frame boundary. The client parser (`lib/title-events.ts:83-91`) splits on real `\n`, skips non-`data: ` lines, and `JSON.parse`s the remainder inside a try/catch — a malformed slice is dropped, not executed. Round-trip is sound.

**2. Cross-user isolation — SAFE.** `userId` is server-derived via `auth:require-user` → `result.user.id` (`title-events.ts:44-57`), never request-supplied. The subscriber filters `payload.userId !== userId` (line 103) and additionally type-guards `conversationId`/`title`. Tested at `__tests__/server/title-events.test.ts:140-147`.

**3. Subscriber / timer leak — SAFE.** `cleanup()` (`title-events.ts:68-76`) is idempotent (`if (closed) return` then `closed = true`), clears the keepalive interval, and unsubscribes by the unique per-connection `subKey`. It is wired to `stream.onClose` (line 78), to `safeWrite`'s catch (line 85), and to the keepalive write-failure catch (line 127). `bus.unsubscribe` matches on the key and is a no-op if absent (verified in `core/src/hook-bus.ts:173-185`). All teardown paths covered; disconnect-cleanup is tested (`title-events.test.ts:149-156`).

**4. Client reconnect — SAFE, bounded.** `lib/title-events.ts:35-58`: a 401 makes `resp.ok` false → throws → caught → backoff before the next attempt, so a persistent 401 reconnects at most every `maxDelayMs` (30s), never a tight loop. `attempt` resets to 0 only after a confirmed open (line 48), so backoff grows correctly across consecutive failures. `stop()` sets `stopped = true` and calls `controller?.abort()`, which aborts the in-flight fetch; the `if (stopped) return` checks at lines 54 and inside the `while` guard end the loop. Reconnect-after-end and onOpen-reset are tested (`title-events.test.ts:39-60`).

**5. applyTitle correctness — CORRECT.** `session-store.ts:142-150`: returns early (stable `state.sessions` reference) on unknown id and on unchanged title; otherwise `slice()` + replace the single index with a new object via `set()`. No mutation, no off-by-one. Reference-stability on both no-op paths is explicitly tested (`session-store-apply-title.test.ts:22-34`).

**6. Fire placement — CORRECT.** `conversations/src/plugin.ts:626-630` fires strictly inside `if (updated)` and `return`s immediately, so it never reaches the ifNull no-op (`updated=false`) or the post-write not-found re-check below. All three cases are tested in `set-title.test.ts:498-581` (fires on real change; not on ifNull no-op; not on not-found).

**7. AX invariants — SATISFIED.** No product-code cross-plugin import: `server/title-events.ts:26-30` and `lib/title-events.ts:8-11` each declare their own local `TitleUpdatedEvent`/`TitleEventFrame` duck-type; the only `@ax/conversations` imports in channel-web are in `__tests__/` harness composition, consistent with existing tests. Payload is domain-level (`conversationId`/`userId`/`title`) with no storage/transport vocabulary (invariant #1). Not half-wired: the route auto-registers in `server/plugin.ts:147-162` and the consumer (`useTitleEvents` in `App.tsx:134`) ships in the same PR (invariant #3). Note: the manifest schema (`core/src/plugin.ts`) has only `registers`/`calls`/`subscribes` — there is no `emits`/`fires` field, and other fired events (`chat:stream-chunk`, `chat:turn-end`) are likewise undeclared, so not declaring `conversations:title-updated` is consistent with the established convention, not a gap.

**8. Test coverage — GOOD, with one minor gap noted below.** Server handler (401 / isolation / frame format / disconnect-cleanup), client consumer (frame parse / junk-skip / reconnect / onOpen), `applyTitle` (3 cases incl. reference stability), the hook (mount/route/unmount), and the producer event (3 cases) are all covered. The new route's registration is verified via the 401-not-404 probe (`__tests__/server/plugin.test.ts`).

---

### Minor (below report threshold, noted for completeness, not blocking)

- **Minor (confidence ~55): no explicit test for the newline/quote injection case.** Risk #1 is the headline security property of this change and the round-trip is correct, but no test feeds a title like `"\n\ndata: forged"` end-to-end (or even just asserts the server frame for such a title). Existing tests use clean titles (`'Hello'`, `'One'`). Suggested add: in `__tests__/server/title-events.test.ts`, fire a title containing `\n\n` and `"` and assert the captured write contains exactly one `\n\n` (the frame terminator) and the escaped sequence, not a second blank-line boundary. Cheap regression guard for the property the design leans on.

- **Nit (confidence ~40): keepalive write-failure path doesn't `stream.close()`.** `title-events.ts:122-129` — the keepalive catch calls `cleanup()` but, unlike `safeWrite` (lines 84-90), does not attempt `stream.close()`. Functionally fine (a write that just threw means the socket is already gone, and `cleanup` stops the timer + unsubscribes, so no leak), and it mirrors the same asymmetry already present in `sse.ts:291-298`, so it's consistent with the existing pattern. Not worth changing.

Net: this is a clean, well-tested, correctly-scoped change. I'd merge it; the only thing I'd genuinely suggest is the newline-injection test as a guard on the property the whole design depends on.

input.content:
## What & why

Closes the `TODO.md` "Live title refresh after the poll window" item. PR #120 widened the client's `pollConversationTitle` window to ~10s, but a title the backend persists *after* that window only surfaced on a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) — there was no server→client push. This adds that push channel over SSE.

**Additive, not replacing:** the existing client poll stays as the fast path for the just-created active thread; the new SSE is a long-lived catch-all that surfaces *any* of the user's conversation titles the moment they're written. The two are idempotent (both set the same value).

## How it works

```
set-title (updated===true)
  → fire conversations:title-updated { conversationId, userId, title }   [in-process bus]
       → GET /api/chat/title-events subscriber (filtered by authed userId)
            → data: { conversationId, title }                            [SSE frame]
                 → client applyTitle() updates the sidebar row in place  [no reload]
```

- **Backend (single source of truth):** `conversations:set-title` — the only title write point — fires a new subscriber event `conversations:title-updated` **only on a real change** (never on the `ifNull` no-op or the not-found path). Covers today's auto-title pipeline and any future rename UI for free.
- **Per-user SSE route** `GET /api/chat/title-events` (channel-web): auth-gated, subscribes to the event filtered by the server-derived `userId`, streams `{conversationId,title}` frames. One connection per tab covers all sidebar rows. Mirrors the existing `/api/chat/stream/:reqId` handler (keepalive + idempotent cleanup); **no replay buffer** — titles live in the DB, the SSE only pushes changes.
- **Client consumer** (`title-events.ts` + `use-title-events.ts`, mounted in `AppContent`): fetch+ReadableStream SSE (not `EventSource` — needs credentials), updates the matching `session-store` row in place via the new `applyTitle`, and runs a `list()` resync on (re)connect to catch anything missed while disconnected. Capped-backoff reconnect; aborts on unmount.

## Design / plan

- Design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-design.md`
- Impl plan: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-impl.md`

## Boundary review (new subscriber event `conversations:title-updated`)

- **Alternate impl:** titles stored in a KV/document backend instead of a SQL column — payload `{conversationId,userId,title}` is unchanged. Holds.
- **Leaky field names:** none — all three are domain-level (no `sha`/`bucket`/`pod`/`socket`).
- **Subscriber risk:** the SSE handler keys off `userId` + `conversationId`, both stable domain ids — nothing backend-specific to break on.
- **Wire surface:** the SSE frame `{conversationId,title}` schema lives in channel-web (route owner), not a central file. No manifest `emits`/`fires` field exists; fired events are undeclared by convention (consistent with `chat:stream-chunk`/`chat:turn-end`). channel-web declares it `subscribes`.

## Security review
- Sandbox: New auth-gated **read-only** route `GET /api/chat/title-events` + in-process event `conversations:title-updated {conversationId,userId,title}`. No FS path / process spawn / env read / opaque handle / caller-supplied params. Subscriber filters on the server-derived authenticated `userId` (never request-supplied), so a connection only ever receives the caller's own titles. Capability bounded.
- Injection: `title` is model-generated (untrusted). Server `JSON.stringify`'s it into `data: …\n\n` — JSON escaping neutralizes newlines/quotes, so a title **cannot forge an SSE frame boundary** (regression-tested). Client `JSON.parse`'s it and renders via React text (no raw-HTML sink); never interpolated into shell/SQL/URL/path/prompt. Same string already rendered via the existing `list()` path — no new injection surface.
- Supply chain: N/A — no `package.json` or `pnpm-lock.yaml` changes (verified empty diff).

## Half-wired window

**CLOSED in this PR** (invariant #3): backend event + route + client consumer all ship together. The route auto-registers inside the channel-web plugin (already in CLI + k8s presets) — no preset/chart change, no new env vars.

## Multi-replica note (deferred, design I6)

The event rides the in-process bus, identical to the existing chat SSE. The host is single-replica today (HPA/multi-replica deferred in the chart). When multi-replica lands, *both* SSEs need the cross-replica `eventbus` (LISTEN/NOTIFY) or session affinity — a shared future concern, not solved here.

## Test plan

- **Backend unit:** `set-title` fires the event only on a real change; silent on the `ifNull` no-op and not-found.
- **Server SSE:** 401 unauth; cross-user isolation (A's title never reaches B's stream); frame format; **frame-boundary injection guard** (newline/quote title round-trips, can't forge a frame); cleanup unsubscribes on disconnect.
- **Client:** consumer parses frames / skips junk / reconnects / fires `onOpen`; `applyTitle` updates the row and no-ops (reference-stable) on unknown id + unchanged title; hook wires actions and stops on unmount; the new route is reachable (live 401-not-404 probe in the plugin integration test).
- **Gate:** `pnpm build` + `pnpm test` + `pnpm lint` all green; whole-branch review clean.
- **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE (kind `ax-next-dev`) — pending:** create a chat, let the title land past the ~10s window with the tab open → sidebar title updates with no reload.

lastPrompt: /yolo-ship write the implementation plan for docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-design.md and then implement it

aiTitle: Add SSE event for live title refresh

content.text:
Here's where PR #122 stands.

## ✅ Shipped to PR — CI green, awaiting CodeRabbit

**PR:** https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/122 — `feat(channel-web): live title refresh via SSE`

**What landed (9 commits):**
- **Backend:** `conversations:set-title` (the single title write point) fires a new `conversations:title-updated` event — only on a real change, never on the `ifNull` no-op or not-found.
- **Server:** per-user SSE route `GET /api/chat/title-events`, auth-gated, filters by the server-derived `userId`, mirrors the existing chat-stream handler.
- **Client:** long-lived consumer in `AppContent` → updates the sidebar row in place via new `session-store.applyTitle` + `list()` resync on connect. Existing poll left untouched (additive, per your choice).

**Quality gates (all green):**
- `pnpm build` ✓ · full `pnpm test` ✓ · `pnpm lint` ✓
- Whole-branch code review: **no blocking issues** across all 8 risk areas (frame injection, user isolation, subscriber/timer leak, reconnect, applyTitle, fire placement, invariants, coverage).
- Added the one suggestion it raised: a regression test proving a malicious newline/quote title can't forge an SSE frame.
- Security checklist walked (bounded read-only capability; model-generated title is JSON-escaped into the frame + React-rendered as text; no new deps).
- **CI: test / Analyze / gitleaks / semgrep all pass.**

**The one open item:** CodeRabbit was **rate-limited** (today's earlier PRs spent the hourly budget) — its "pass" check is a false signal; it never actually reviewed. Refill is **02:09Z**. I've scheduled a wakeup at ~02:13Z to trigger `@coderabbitai review`, address anything it finds, and confirm green — then I'll report back. I won't merge.

A couple of things logged for you:
- The TODO item is marked done (referencing the design doc rather than the PR number, to avoid a no-op push that'd burn a CodeRabbit review).
- Worktree base was `origin/main`, so your two unpushed local commits (`f1e2deb7` spacing, `c168fb8c` auth baseURL) are **not** in this PR — they're still on your local `main` to push separately.

I'll resume automatically after the CodeRabbit refill.

45	# Brainstorming session scratch (mockups, state) — kept local
46	.superpowers/

1	# TODO — deferred work
2	
3	Living list of work explicitly punted, parked, or "wait until earned." Anything
4	without a concrete trigger is a candidate for deletion. When an item ships,
5	strike it through (or remove it) and reference the closing PR.
6	
7	Sources scanned (2026-05-19): `MEMORY.md`, recent `docs/plans/*followup*`,
8	`docs/plans/2026-05-10-memory-strata-roadmap.md`,
9	`docs/plans/2026-05-08-first-use-onboarding-followup.md`, recent commits, and
10	`project_codex_findings_2026_04_29.md`.
11	
12	---
13	
14	## Routines
15	
16	- [ ] **Default routines for team agents (fire-under-team policy).** Spun out of the PR #105 fix: `agents:list-personal-owners` excludes team agents because routing a default fire under "the team" needs a policy answer — does the routine fire under each member separately, under the team creator, under a designated steward? Pick up when team agents become a real surface; for now, team-owned agents simply don't get default routines materialized.
17	- [ ] **Cron + webhook triggers for default routines.** Deferred per I-R5 / HP7. Cron needs a croner evaluator in claim SQL; webhook needs per-default tokens + live rebind on admin edit. Pick up when a real caller needs it.
18	- [ ] **Phase F — conversation titles.** Branch parked. New piece of work, not a follow-up.
19	- [ ] **Per-team / per-tenant scoped default routines.** Not currently load-bearing.
20	- [ ] **Per-agent opt-out for default routines** beyond the current override-by-name mechanic.
21	- [ ] **"Drift indicator" UI** (visibility into stale `definition_updated_at`).
22	- [ ] **Default → workspace "promote" flow.**
23	
24	## Skills (Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96)
25	
26	- [ ] **System-prompt fold for skill descriptions** (Phase A — **PARKED**). SDK-only today (SDK indexes description into prompt + invokes `Skill` tool on demand). Formally parked in `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-capability-lifecycle-impl.md` — trigger to un-park is either `packages/agent-native-runner/` gaining `src/` or `packages/test-harness/` adding a real-LLM code path. No code lands until then; when the trigger fires, write a fresh `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-skills-system-prompt-fold-impl.md`.
27	
28	## Attachments / artifacts
29	
30	- [ ] **`$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/sessions/` mirroring.** Today's scaffold only links `projects/`. Add a sibling symlink if a future feature needs session metadata in the workspace.
31	
32	## Auth / onboarding cleanup (Phase 4 + Phase 5 follow-ups)
33	
34	These wait on stated triggers — don't ship pre-emptively.
35	
36	- [ ] **`ax admin reset-password` CLI + `/auth/reset-password` route.** Pick up when local password sign-in becomes a real surface (wizard re-adds password OR Settings → Password lands). Today the wizard captures only name+email against `@ax/auth-better` (which silently ignores the optional `password` input until the local-password slice lands).
37	- [ ] **Walk the Google new-user gate-*allow* branch live.** The PR #116 `kind` walk used the existing admin email, so it exercised account-LINKING + the cookie bridge + the gate's reject branch — but NOT brand-new-user creation (gate allows an in-domain email → creates a `role:'user'` row with the Google-supplied name). That path is unit-tested only. Pick up next kind walk with a second `@<allowed-domain>` Google account on hand.
38	- [ ] **Port the gated `k8s-e2e` tests off `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/k8s-e2e/helpers.ts:42` + `credentials-admin-roundtrip.test.ts` still POST to the retired route. Gated by `AX_K8S_E2E=1`, not in CI, so this didn't block the retirement merge — but anyone walking those e2e tests against `kind-ax-next-dev` will hit immediate failures. Fix: rewrite `signInAsK8sAdmin` to call `auth:create-bootstrap-user` over the bus (or POST `/setup/admin` via the wizard) and exchange for the signed cookie, mirroring `@ax/test-harness/sign-in.ts`. Pick up next time someone walks the e2e suite.
39	- [ ] **Refresh `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` dev-bootstrap caveat.** The walk-through document references "the dev-bootstrap auth path mints a single shared user with `role='admin'`" — no longer accurate after the retirement. Update next time the doc gets walked.
40	
41	## Credentials UX redesign (PR #109, merged 2026-05-19)
42	
43	- [ ] **User-delete wiring in `@ax/auth-better`.** When a user-delete service hook lands, it should call `credentials:purge-by-owner({ scope: 'user', ownerId })` — the facade hook + the matching agent-delete wiring already exist; only the auth-better side is missing.
44	- [ ] **Bulk "all credentials" admin inventory view.** Design §3 non-goal; revisit when an operator needs a single audit surface.
45	
46	## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)
47	
48	Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.
49	
50	- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** — when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
51	- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** — when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
52	- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** — only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
53	- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** — when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
54	- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** — explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
55	- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** — Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".
56	
57	## UI Bugs
58	
59	- [x] ~~Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats~~ — fixed (PR #120): bounded retry replaces the one-shot title gate (`@ax/conversation-titles`) + widened `generateTitle` poll window (`channel-web`).
60	- [x] ~~When files are attached to a user message, that message doesn't show the attachment chip when loaded again from a previous chat session~~ — fixed (PR #120): the runner-translated text-mention is reconstructed back into an `attachment` block on the `conversations:get` read path, gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix.
61	
62	### UI bug follow-ups
63	
64	- [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.
65	
66	## Web tools (@ax/web-tools, PR for `feat/web-tools-impl`)
67	
68	- [ ] **Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.
69	- [ ] **Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.

65: - [x] ~~**Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel.~~ — fixed: the title write point (`conversations:set-title`) fires `conversations:title-updated`; a per-user SSE (`GET /api/chat/title-events`) pushes it to the sidebar (live in-place row update + `list()` resync on connect). The ~10s client poll stays as the active-thread fast path. Design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-design.md`.
66: =======
67: - [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.
68: 
69: ## Web tools (@ax/web-tools, PR for `feat/web-tools-impl`)
70: 
71: - [ ] **Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.
72: - [ ] **Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.
73: - [ ] **Two unrelated fixes parked on `feat/web-tools` (NOT in the web-tools PR).** `b3c87929` (`fix(auth-better): thread AX_PUBLIC_BASE_URL into better-auth baseURL`) and `494b9e7c` (`fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill`) rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch but are unrelated to web tools, so they were dropped from `feat/web-tools-impl` (rebased `--onto origin/main`) to keep the PR scoped. They are preserved on the untouched `feat/web-tools` branch — ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick.

content.text:
Done — both the push and the downstream cleanup it triggered.

## ✅ Pushed your two local `main` commits

`origin/main` had moved while we worked (web-tools **#121** merged), so a plain push was non-fast-forward. I:
1. Probed for conflicts (clean — the `preset` hunks don't overlap).
2. Rebased your two commits onto the new `origin/main` (no conflicts), and verified the rebased `presets/k8s` diff is coherent additive wiring next to #121.
3. Fast-forward pushed: `42fc4af8..3d24f597 main -> main`. Local and `origin/main` are in sync.

The commits are now on `origin/main` as `3d24f597` (channel-web spacing) and `44087f24` (auth baseURL). The main checkout is untouched — it's on your `feat/agent-ephemeral-scratch` WIP branch, and `main` wasn't checked out anywhere, so I could rebase it safely from this worktree.

## ⚠️ Side effect I had to fix: PR #122 went `CONFLICTING`

Because `main` advanced (now has #121 + your two commits), PR #122 (based on `main`) conflicted on `TODO.md`. I merged `main` into the PR branch and resolved it (kept the struck live-title item **and** main's new web-tools section; `.gitignore` auto-merged). **PR #122 is back to `MERGEABLE`/`CLEAN`** and CI is re-running on the merge commit.

## Two things for you

- **Stale TODO item:** the web-tools TODO now says those two fixes are "parked … ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick" — but you just shipped them to `main`. I left it untouched to keep PR #122 scoped. Want me to strike it (small direct commit on `main`)?
- **PR #122 status:** CI green on the prior commit; re-running now on the merge. CodeRabbit is still rate-limited until **02:09Z** — my scheduled wakeup (~02:13Z) will trigger its review, verify CI on the merge commit, address any findings, and report back. I won't merge.

Striking it directly on `main` is the cleanest fulfillment (corrects `main` immediately, keeps PR #122 scoped) — and a 3-way merge analysis confirms it won't re-conflict PR #122 (the PR branch leaves that line at base, so main's change just wins on merge). Let me check out `main`, read the exact item, and strike it.

# TODO — deferred work

Living list of work explicitly punted, parked, or "wait until earned." Anything
without a concrete trigger is a candidate for deletion. When an item ships,
strike it through (or remove it) and reference the closing PR.

Sources scanned (2026-05-19): `MEMORY.md`, recent `docs/plans/*followup*`,
`docs/plans/2026-05-10-memory-strata-roadmap.md`,
`docs/plans/2026-05-08-first-use-onboarding-followup.md`, recent commits, and
`project_codex_findings_2026_04_29.md`.

---

## Routines

- [ ] **Default routines for team agents (fire-under-team policy).** Spun out of the PR #105 fix: `agents:list-personal-owners` excludes team agents because routing a default fire under "the team" needs a policy answer — does the routine fire under each member separately, under the team creator, under a designated steward? Pick up when team agents become a real surface; for now, team-owned agents simply don't get default routines materialized.
- [ ] **Cron + webhook triggers for default routines.** Deferred per I-R5 / HP7. Cron needs a croner evaluator in claim SQL; webhook needs per-default tokens + live rebind on admin edit. Pick up when a real caller needs it.
- [ ] **Phase F — conversation titles.** Branch parked. New piece of work, not a follow-up.
- [ ] **Per-team / per-tenant scoped default routines.** Not currently load-bearing.
- [ ] **Per-agent opt-out for default routines** beyond the current override-by-name mechanic.
- [ ] **"Drift indicator" UI** (visibility into stale `definition_updated_at`).
- [ ] **Default → workspace "promote" flow.**

## Skills (Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96)

- [ ] **System-prompt fold for skill descriptions** (Phase A — **PARKED**). SDK-only today (SDK indexes description into prompt + invokes `Skill` tool on demand). Formally parked in `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-capability-lifecycle-impl.md` — trigger to un-park is either `packages/agent-native-runner/` gaining `src/` or `packages/test-harness/` adding a real-LLM code path. No code lands until then; when the trigger fires, write a fresh `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-skills-system-prompt-fold-impl.md`.

## Attachments / artifacts

- [ ] **`$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/sessions/` mirroring.** Today's scaffold only links `projects/`. Add a sibling symlink if a future feature needs session metadata in the workspace.

## Auth / onboarding cleanup (Phase 4 + Phase 5 follow-ups)

These wait on stated triggers — don't ship pre-emptively.

- [ ] **`ax admin reset-password` CLI + `/auth/reset-password` route.** Pick up when local password sign-in becomes a real surface (wizard re-adds password OR Settings → Password lands). Today the wizard captures only name+email against `@ax/auth-better` (which silently ignores the optional `password` input until the local-password slice lands).
- [ ] **Walk the Google new-user gate-*allow* branch live.** The PR #116 `kind` walk used the existing admin email, so it exercised account-LINKING + the cookie bridge + the gate's reject branch — but NOT brand-new-user creation (gate allows an in-domain email → creates a `role:'user'` row with the Google-supplied name). That path is unit-tested only. Pick up next kind walk with a second `@<allowed-domain>` Google account on hand.
- [ ] **Port the gated `k8s-e2e` tests off `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/k8s-e2e/helpers.ts:42` + `credentials-admin-roundtrip.test.ts` still POST to the retired route. Gated by `AX_K8S_E2E=1`, not in CI, so this didn't block the retirement merge — but anyone walking those e2e tests against `kind-ax-next-dev` will hit immediate failures. Fix: rewrite `signInAsK8sAdmin` to call `auth:create-bootstrap-user` over the bus (or POST `/setup/admin` via the wizard) and exchange for the signed cookie, mirroring `@ax/test-harness/sign-in.ts`. Pick up next time someone walks the e2e suite.
- [ ] **Refresh `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` dev-bootstrap caveat.** The walk-through document references "the dev-bootstrap auth path mints a single shared user with `role='admin'`" — no longer accurate after the retirement. Update next time the doc gets walked.

## Credentials UX redesign (PR #109, merged 2026-05-19)

- [ ] **User-delete wiring in `@ax/auth-better`.** When a user-delete service hook lands, it should call `credentials:purge-by-owner({ scope: 'user', ownerId })` — the facade hook + the matching agent-delete wiring already exist; only the auth-better side is missing.
- [ ] **Bulk "all credentials" admin inventory view.** Design §3 non-goal; revisit when an operator needs a single audit surface.

## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)

Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.

- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** — when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** — when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** — only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** — when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** — explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** — Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".

## UI Bugs

- [x] ~~Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats~~ — fixed (PR #120): bounded retry replaces the one-shot title gate (`@ax/conversation-titles`) + widened `generateTitle` poll window (`channel-web`).
- [x] ~~When files are attached to a user message, that message doesn't show the attachment chip when loaded again from a previous chat session~~ — fixed (PR #120): the runner-translated text-mention is reconstructed back into an `attachment` block on the `conversations:get` read path, gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix.

### UI bug follow-ups

- [x] ~~**Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel.~~ — fixed: the title write point (`conversations:set-title`) fires `conversations:title-updated`; a per-user SSE (`GET /api/chat/title-events`) pushes it to the sidebar (live in-place row update + `list()` resync on connect). The ~10s client poll stays as the active-thread fast path. Design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-design.md`.

## Web tools (@ax/web-tools, PR for `feat/web-tools-impl`)

- [ ] **Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.
- [ ] **Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.
- [ ] **Two unrelated fixes parked on `feat/web-tools` (NOT in the web-tools PR).** `b3c87929` (`fix(auth-better): thread AX_PUBLIC_BASE_URL into better-auth baseURL`) and `494b9e7c` (`fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill`) rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch but are unrelated to web tools, so they were dropped from `feat/web-tools-impl` (rebased `--onto origin/main`) to keep the PR scoped. They are preserved on the untouched `feat/web-tools` branch — ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick.
- [ ] **Web-tools cost/usage metering + per-agent search-spend visibility.** YAGNI now (plan "Out of scope"). Add a usage-metering subscriber hook if operators need per-agent web-search spend (web search bills ~$10/1k). Trigger: an operator asks for the visibility.

########## docs/plans/2026-05-21-live-title-refresh-sse-design.md:42 ##########
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Add language identifiers to fenced code blocks to satisfy markdown lint.**

Both fences are missing a language tag (MD040), which can fail docs lint.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-```
+```text
 conversations:title-updated  →  { conversationId: string; userId: string; title: string }
 ```

-```
+```text
 assistant turn ends
   → `@ax/conversation-titles` (chat:turn-end subscriber) generates title
   → conversations:set-title  (updated === true)
        → fire conversations:title-updated { conversationId, userId, title }   [in-process bus]
             → GET /api/chat/title-events subscriber (filtered by userId)
                  → data: { conversationId, title }                            [SSE frame]
                       → client updates session-store row + thread-list title  [no reload]
 ```
```
</details>


Also applies to: 93-101

The fenced code blocks in this file lack language identifiers causing MD040 lint
failures; update both fences around the event/doc snippets to include a language
tag (e.g., ```text) so the blocks with lines like "conversations:title-updated 
→  { conversationId: string; userId: string; title: string }" and the longer

########## packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:630 ##########
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Avoid emitting `conversations:title-updated` on idempotent same-title writes.**

Line 618 currently uses `updated` as the fire condition, but `updated` can be true even when the title value is unchanged. That can publish duplicate events for no-op rewrites.

<details>
<summary>💡 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-  if (updated) {
+  if (updated && conv.title !== title) {
     await bus.fire('conversations:title-updated', ctx, {
       conversationId: input.conversationId,
       userId: input.userId,
       title,
     } satisfies TitleUpdatedEvent);
-    return { updated: true };
   }
+  if (updated) return { updated: true };
```
</details>

Please also add a regression test in `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/set-title.test.ts` for “same title written twice” asserting no second event is fired.
 
As per coding guidelines `**/*.test.{ts,tsx}`: “Whenever you fix a bug that wasn't caught by an existing test, you MUST add a test that would have caught it first.”

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts` around lines 619 - 630, The code
currently uses the boolean updated to decide whether to fire
bus.fire('conversations:title-updated', ...) but updated can be true for
idempotent writes; change the logic to compute a true "changed" flag by
comparing the prior title to the new title (e.g., previousTitle !== title,
applying any normalization used elsewhere), set updated = changed, and only call
bus.fire(...) (the TitleUpdatedEvent payload containing conversationId, userId,
title) when changed is true; also add a regression test in the set-title tests
called "same title written twice" that writes the same title twice and asserts
the event bus/fire spy is invoked only once.
```

</details>

content.text:
CodeRabbit posted 2 Minor findings. Both are legitimate — let me evaluate with receiving-code-review rigor:

1. **`plugin.ts:630`** — fire on idempotent same-title writes. **Valid latent bug:** `updated` is row-matched (rowCount), so a no-`ifNull` rewrite to the *same* title (the future rename path) would set `updated=true` and re-fire. Worth guarding + a regression test (Bug Fix Policy).
2. **design doc fences (MD040)** — missing language tags. Trivial, valid markdown nit (not in CI lint, but cheap to clean).

Let me verify the code facts before fixing — `conv.title` scope, what `create` sets as the initial title, and check both docs for untagged fences.

# Live title refresh via SSE

**Date:** 2026-05-21
**Status:** Design — approved, not yet implemented.
**Context:** `TODO.md` → "Live title refresh after the poll window." PR #120
widened the client-side `pollConversationTitle` window to ~10s, but a title
that the backend persists *after* that window still doesn't surface until a
sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) — there's no server→client push
channel. The TODO names the real fix: a title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or
a low-frequency background `list()` poll. We pick SSE, following the existing
`GET /api/chat/stream/:reqId` precedent.

## Decision (approved)

**Additive, not replacing.** The existing client poll stays as the fast path
for the just-created active thread. We add a long-lived **per-user** SSE that
surfaces *any* of the user's conversation titles the moment the backend writes
them. The two paths are idempotent — both set the same value — so overlap is
harmless. We deliberately do **not** rip out the poll (smaller blast radius;
leaves the working assistant-ui `generateTitle` integration untouched).

We push titles over **Server-Sent Events**, not websockets or a background
poll: SSE is already the project's server→client streaming transport
(`/api/chat/stream/:reqId`), it's one-directional (which is all we need), and
it reuses `http-server`'s `res.stream()` + the per-connection bus-subscriber
pattern verbatim.

## Architecture

Three units, each independently testable:

### 1. `conversations:title-updated` — new subscriber event (single source of truth)

`setConversationTitle` (`packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts`) is the **only**
write point for a conversation title, and it already computes
`updated: boolean`. We fire a new subscriber event on the in-process bus
**only when `updated === true`** (a real change), immediately before
`return { updated: true }`:

```
conversations:title-updated  →  { conversationId: string; userId: string; title: string }
```

- **Silent on no-ops.** Not fired when `ifNull=true` hits an already-titled row,
  nor on the not-found re-check path. Subscribers only ever see real changes.
- **Covers every caller.** Today that's `@ax/conversation-titles`' auto-title
  pipeline; a future user-driven rename UI also goes through `set-title`, so it
  gets live multi-tab sync for free.
- The conversations manifest gains the fired event in its declaration; the
  channel-web manifest declares it `subscribes` to it.

### 2. `GET /api/chat/title-events` — per-user SSE route (channel-web)

Per-**user**, not per-conversation: the sidebar wants updates for *any* of the
user's conversations over **one** connection, not one connection per row. The
handler mirrors `createSseHandler` (`packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts`):

1. `auth:require-user` → `userId`. 401 on rejection (route closed by default).
2. Open `text/event-stream; charset=utf-8` via `res.stream()`.
3. Subscribe to `conversations:title-updated` with a per-connection key
   (`@ax/channel-web/title-events/<userId>-<rand>`), filtering
   `payload.userId === userId`. A user never sees another user's titles.
4. Per matching event, write one frame:
   `data: {"conversationId":"…","title":"…"}\n\n`.
5. 25s `:\n\n` keepalive (unref'd interval); `stream.onClose` → unsubscribe +
   clear timer. Cleanup is idempotent.

**No replay buffer** (unlike chat chunks). Titles live in the DB and the
initial `list()` on page load already renders current state; the SSE only needs
to push *changes* that happen while connected. A frame missed during a
disconnect is recovered by the client's resync-on-connect (unit 3), so we don't
carry the buffer/eviction machinery the chat stream needs.

### 3. Long-lived client consumer (channel-web)

A single SSE connection opened once at app/sidebar mount, using
`fetch` + `ReadableStream` exactly like `transport.ts`
(`credentials: 'include'`, `accept: text/event-stream`) — **not** a raw
`EventSource`, which can't send credentials cleanly:

- Each parsed `{ conversationId, title }` frame → update the matching
  `SessionRow.title` in `session-store` (and the assistant-ui thread-list
  title). An unknown `conversationId` is ignored (not in this user's loaded
  list, or already removed).
- **Resync on (re)connect:** run `list()` once each time the stream opens, to
  catch any title that landed while disconnected. This is what lets us skip a
  server-side replay buffer.
- **Reconnect** with capped exponential backoff; **abort** on unmount.
- The existing `pollConversationTitle` is **unchanged**.

## Data flow

```
assistant turn ends
  → @ax/conversation-titles (chat:turn-end subscriber) generates title
  → conversations:set-title  (updated === true)
       → fire conversations:title-updated { conversationId, userId, title }   [in-process bus]
            → GET /api/chat/title-events subscriber (filtered by userId)
                 → data: { conversationId, title }                            [SSE frame]
                      → client updates session-store row + thread-list title  [no reload]
```

## Invariants (carried from review history)

- **I1 — Single source of truth (invariant #4).** The event fires from the
  `set-title` write point, never from `@ax/conversation-titles`. One write →
  one signal, regardless of caller.
- **I2 — Fire only on real change.** No event on the `ifNull` no-op or
  not-found path. A test asserts silence on both.
- **I3 — Transport/storage-agnostic payload (invariant #1).** Payload is
  `{ conversationId, userId, title }` — all domain concepts, no `sha`/`bucket`/
  `pod`/`socket` leakage.
- **I4 — User isolation.** The SSE subscriber filters `payload.userId ===
  userId` (userId derived server-side from `auth:require-user`, never from the
  request). A cross-user isolation test is mandatory.
- **I5 — No half-wired window (invariant #3).** Backend event + route + client
  consumer ship in one PR. The route auto-registers inside the channel-web
  plugin, already loaded in the CLI and k8s presets — no preset edit needed.
- **I6 — Single-replica caveat (documented, deferred).** The event rides the
  in-process bus, identical to chat-stream. The host is `replicas: 1` today
  (HPA/multi-replica explicitly deferred in the chart). When multi-replica
  lands, *both* SSEs need the cross-replica `eventbus` (LISTEN/NOTIFY) or
  session affinity — a shared future concern, not solved here (YAGNI).

## Boundary review (new subscriber event)

- **Alternate impl this event could have:** titles stored in a KV/document
  backend instead of a SQL column — the event payload is unchanged
  (`{ conversationId, userId, title }`), so the abstraction holds.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. All three fields are
  domain-level.
- **Subscriber risk:** the SSE handler keys off `userId` and `conversationId`,
  both stable domain identifiers — no backend-specific field to break on.
- **Wire surface:** the SSE frame `{ conversationId, title }` is a wire shape;
  its schema lives in channel-web (where the route lives), not a central file.

## Security note (see `security-checklist` skill, run at implementation)

- Auth-gated (`auth:require-user`), read-only, and user-filtered — a connection
  only ever receives the caller's own titles.
- Title text is model-generated (untrusted), but it is already rendered to the
  sidebar via `list()` as plain React text content (no raw-HTML injection sink);
  the SSE introduces **no new injection surface**.
- The `conversations:title-updated` payload carries model output across the bus;
  the SSE handler treats it as data and JSON-encodes it into the frame.

## Testing

- **Backend unit (conversations):** `set-title` fires `conversations:title-updated`
  with the correct payload **only** when `updated === true`; silent on the
  `ifNull` already-titled no-op and on not-found. (Bug-fix/new-behavior policy.)
- **Backend SSE (channel-web):** 401 when unauthenticated; cross-user isolation
  (user A's title never reaches user B's stream); frame format; cleanup
  unsubscribes on client disconnect. Mirror `sse.test` patterns.
- **Frontend (channel-web):** consumer updates the matching row, ignores an
  unknown `conversationId`, runs `list()` resync on connect, and reconnects with
  backoff. Mirror `transport` test patterns.
- **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE (kind `ax-next-dev`):** create a chat, let the title land
  *past* the ~10s poll window with the tab open → the sidebar title updates with
  **no reload**.

## Out of scope

- Replacing the client poll (chose additive).
- Cross-replica delivery (rides the future chat-stream multi-replica fix).
- A general-purpose per-user "conversation events" channel (rename/delete/new) —
  this is title-only; broaden it when a second event-type actually needs it.
